title: fix(deployer): resolve .js tsconfig aliases during build

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(deployer): support tsconfig paths when using extended configs
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relevance: Related prior fix to the same tsconfig-paths plugin, but for extended config support not .js extensions

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix: resolve TypeScript path aliases in transpiled workspace packages
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relevance: Related path alias resolution fix but for transpiled workspace packages, different scope

relatedIssues.title: Deployer tsConfigPaths plugin does not resolve path aliases during build

summary: Fixes an issue where the deployer's tsconfig-paths Rollup plugin fails to resolve ESM-style `.js` module specifiers (e.g., `~/utils/build-flags.js`) that point to TypeScript source files. Adds a `resolveJsImportToSourceFile` function that, when a `.js` import doesn't exist on disk, tries `.ts`, `.tsx`, `.js`, `.jsx` extensions to find the actual source file. Includes a unit test for the plugin, e2e test updates (with a new fixture file and two new e2e assertions), a doc update to the configuration reference, and a changeset.
maintainerNotes: High-quality PR: well-scoped bug fix with clear description, linked issue (#12448), unit test, e2e regression tests, docs update, and changeset. All CI checks pass and there are no merge conflicts. The e2e test refactors `buildFixture()` into a lazy/cached helper to avoid building in the top-level beforeAll (now deferred to the build describe block). CodeRabbit suggests also handling `.mts`/`.cts` extensions — minor nitpick that could be addressed separately. Related PRs #11788 and #12239 fixed different aspects of tsconfig path resolution and do not overlap with this fix.
